input.content:
Biogas plant BGA2 mainly processes slurry. A direct dosing unit feeds 50 tonnes of mixed substrate per day into fermenter F1. A slurry pre-pit measuring 4 by 8 metres holds 10 cubic metres and pumps the slurry daily into F1 as well.

Fermenter F1 has a diameter of 23 metres and a wall height of 6 metres, equipped with three paddle agitators. It carries a dome roof that also serves as gas storage; the total gas space (dome plus unfilled headspace) is 549 cubic metres. The fermenter operates mesophilically at 40 degrees Celsius with a fill level of 90 percent. Digestate flows from F1 into secondary digester N1, which also measures 23 metres in diameter and 6 metres in height and is fitted with one paddle agitator and one long-axis agitator. N1 also has a dome roof with a total gas space of 549 cubic metres and operates at 40 degrees Celsius. From the secondary digester the digestate moves into digestate store G1 with a diameter of 30 metres and a height of 6 metres, equipped with two submersible agitators. The digestate store is unheated at approximately 20 degrees Celsius and has a total gas space of 924 cubic metres.

The biogas produced is fed to a combined heat and power unit (CHP) with a rated electrical output of 250 kW. The CHP achieves an electrical efficiency of 40 percent and a thermal efficiency of 45 percent. An emergency flare is available for fault conditions. Both a condensate shaft and a leachate shaft are connected to fermenter F1.

Substrate feed: 50 t/d maize silage via the solid feeder into F1 and 10 m³/d cattle slurry from the pre-pit into F1.

scope.feed_topology_note: Feed via Feedstock/Q_substrates, no simulated edge; not scored

dropped.reason: Feed side / ancillaries — input via Feedstock/Q_substrates; not simulated

must_not_invent: second fermenter F2 (plant has only F1)

metadata.topology: primary -> secondary -> storage; CHP power generation; emergency flare
